chapter: Chapter: When People Rebel – 1857 and After

questions.question: Why were the sepoys unhappy in the British army before the Revolt of 1857?
questions.answer: They were unhappy due to low pay, discrimination and religious concerns.

questions.question: What was the main issue with the new rifle cartridges introduced in 1857?
questions.answer: They were believed to be greased with cow and pig fat.

questions.question: Why did the rebels choose Bahadur Shah Zafar as their leader?
questions.answer: He was the Mughal emperor and a symbol of authority.

questions.question: Why did the revolt spread quickly to other regions?
questions.answer: Many groups such as sepoys, peasants and rulers were unhappy with British rule.

questions.question: What role did Rani Lakshmibai play in the revolt?
questions.answer: She led the rebellion in Jhansi and fought against British forces.

questions.question: How did Nana Sahib participate in the revolt?
questions.answer: He led the rebellion in Kanpur.

questions.question: Why did many peasants support the revolt?
questions.answer: They were affected by heavy taxes and British policies.

questions.question: What difficulty did the rebels face during the revolt?
questions.answer: They lacked unity and modern weapons.

questions.question: Why did the Revolt of 1857 fail?
questions.answer: It failed due to lack of coordination and stronger British military power.

questions.question: How did the British suppress the revolt?
questions.answer: They used powerful armies and recaptured important cities.

questions.question: What happened to many rebel leaders after the revolt?
questions.answer: They were executed, imprisoned or exiled.

questions.question: Why did the British end the rule of the East India Company?
questions.answer: Because the revolt showed weaknesses in Company rule.

questions.question: What major administrative change happened after the revolt?
questions.answer: India came under direct rule of the British Crown.

questions.question: How did the British change the army after the revolt?
questions.answer: They increased the number of European soldiers.

questions.question: Why did the British become cautious about interfering in religion?
questions.answer: They feared it might cause another rebellion.

questions.question: Why did some Indian rulers support the revolt?
questions.answer: They were unhappy with British annexation policies.

questions.question: What role did local leaders play in the revolt?
questions.answer: They organized resistance and encouraged people to fight.

questions.question: How did the revolt affect British policies in India?
questions.answer: The British became more careful in governance.

questions.question: Why is the Revolt of 1857 considered a major historical event?
questions.answer: It was the first large uprising against British rule.

questions.question: What message did the revolt send to the British rulers?
questions.answer: It showed strong resistance against colonial rule.

questions.question: Why did the revolt involve different groups of society?
questions.answer: Many groups were dissatisfied with British policies.

questions.question: What impact did the revolt have on Indian nationalism?
questions.answer: It inspired later movements for independence.

questions.question: Why did the rebels lack coordination?
questions.answer: They had no central leadership or unified plan.

questions.question: What lesson did the British learn from the revolt?
questions.answer: They needed stronger control over administration and the army.

questions.question: Why is the Revolt of 1857 studied in Indian history?
questions.answer: It marks an early struggle against colonial rule.
